explanations.en: This action ranks first because it fits the city's preferred short-to-medium timeframe and has very strong policy, legal, and mitigation feasibility support. Its main drawback is that the expected emissions reduction is limited and it does not match the city's preferred sector.

This action stays near the top because it also fits the city's preferred timeframe and has strong policy support, plus it supports the city's preferred co-benefits well. It ranks below the first action because its emissions reduction is limited and it does not match the preferred sector.

This action is placed third because it fits the city's preferred timeframe, has very strong policy support, and supports the city's preferred co-benefits well. It sits below the top two because its expected emissions reduction is very limited and it does not match the preferred sector.

This action ranks fourth because it matches the city's preferred sector and timeframe, with strong policy support, very supportive legal feasibility, and strong co-benefits. It does not rank higher because its expected emissions reduction is very limited in the current city inventory.

This action is kept in the upper middle because it fits the city's preferred timeframe and has very strong policy, legal, and mitigation feasibility support. It ranks below the actions above it because it does not directly match a subsector with recorded city emissions and does not match the preferred sector.

This action ranks sixth because it matches the city's preferred sector and timeframe, with very strong policy support, strong co-benefits, and very strong mitigation feasibility. It is held back by its very limited expected emissions reduction.

This action ranks seventh because it fits the city's preferred timeframe and has strong policy support, strong co-benefits, very supportive legal feasibility, and very strong mitigation feasibility. It does not rank higher because its expected emissions reduction is very limited and it does not match the preferred sector.

This action is lower in the list because, although it fits the city's preferred timeframe and has very strong mitigation feasibility, its policy support is weak and it does not match the preferred sector. Its expected emissions reduction is also limited.

It is limited by the lack of a direct match to recorded city emissions and by not matching the preferred sector.

It does not rank higher because it does not directly match a subsector with recorded city emissions and does not match the preferred sector.

This action is lower because it fits the city's preferred timeframe and has strong policy support, strong co-benefits, supportive legal feasibility, and very strong mitigation feasibility. Its expected emissions reduction is very limited, and it does not match the preferred sector.
